---
title: "between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_aa"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-17
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [26, 20, 17, 17, 20]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot (A-site, P-site, E-site, left-to-right) showing per-amino-acid log2 fold-change of day_5-vs-day_0 median per-replicate frequency with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint. Bars sorted by log2_FC high-to-low within each panel; blue = enriched at day_5 (log2FC >= 0), red = depleted at day_5 (log2FC < 0). Y-axis range ~-0.7 to +0.65.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d5_vs_d0/composite/APE_aa_Day5_vs_Day0_barplot_composite.png)

Each panel ranks the 20 amino acids by signed log2_FC (day_5 median / day_0 median) at one ribosome site. Bars above zero are day_5-enriched relative to day_0; bars below zero are day_5-depleted. Dynamic range is comparable to the d10_vs_d5 sister (~+/-0.5) but the panel-level signal is shallower — site P min `p_adj` here is the only sub-1 cell.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum (two-sided)
- **Design:** n=4 day_5 (BWM_d5_rep2/3 + control_d5_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_0 (BWM_d0_rep2/3 + control_d0_rep2/3); BWM and control reps pooled inside each timepoint; two-sided MW on per-replicate AA frequencies; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site = own family of 20 amino-acid hypotheses); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_d5 / median_d0).

### Family-level caveats

- **mw-floor-blocking** — MW two-sided p-floor for n=4 vs n=4 is exactly 0.02857; with per-site BH-FDR families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon), clearing FDR<0.05 is mathematically impossible unless ~every test ties at the floor. Empirically: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 in any of the 6 files.
- **condition-pooled-confound** — n=4 per timepoint is built by pooling 2 BWM + 2 control reps; if BWM and control diverge in their time response, pooling masks divergent signals. All 6 members share this design.
- **n=4-low-power** — n=4 vs n=4 MW has very low power; no-signal results across all 6 files are weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline** — Headlines must explicitly state min raw p ~= 0.0286 and explain why "no FDR hits" here is uninformative, not a biological negative.

---

## Headline

No statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 (0/60) for AA-level day_5 vs day_0 MW with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint (n=4 per side); min raw p = 0.0286 (the n=4 vs n=4 MW exact floor). One floor row across the file: P-site C (+0.249, raw p=0.0286, p_adj=0.571), with a P-site H near-floor companion (-0.144, raw p=0.0571, p_adj=0.571). Site E is completely flat (min p_adj=1.0); site A min p_adj=0.8. The day_5 vs day_0 contrast carries the *least* signal of the three pairwise contrasts in this family — every site has weaker signal here than in the corresponding d10_vs_d5 or d10_vs_d0 file. The only feature with even a floor-level signal is P-site Cysteine, also flagged at d10_vs_d0_aa. 'No FDR hits' here is structural — the locked n=4 floor caveat, not a biological negative; treat as exploratory.

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` (day_5 / day_0 median ratio). Two p-value columns are shown: `p_value` is the raw two-sided Mann-Whitney p; `p_adj` is the BH-corrected value within its per-site family of 20 amino-acid tests. Each site group is split into one sub-table per sign of effect: positive `log2_FC` means day_5-enriched, negative means day_5-depleted. Up to 5 rows per direction, ranked by raw `p_value` ascending; ties broken by `|log2_FC|` descending. Sites are shown in A/P/E order.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (day_5 > day_0)

| amino acid        | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---               | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| I (isoleucine)    | +0.188             | 0.1143            | 0.800                |      |
| N (asparagine)    | +0.618             | 0.2               | 0.800                |      |
| D (aspartate)     | +0.222             | 0.2               | 0.800                |      |
| E (glutamate)     | +0.069             | 0.3429            | 0.857                |      |
| Q (glutamine)     | +0.056             | 0.3429            | 0.857                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (day_5 < day_0)

| amino acid        | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---               | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| Y (tyrosine)      | -0.412             | 0.1143            | 0.800                |      |
| W (tryptophan)    | -0.671             | 0.2               | 0.800                |      |
| L (leucine)       | -0.097             | 0.3429            | 0.857                |      |
| A (alanine)       | -0.243             | 0.4857            | 0.883                |      |
| P (proline)       | -0.074             | 0.4857            | 0.883                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (day_5 > day_0)

| amino acid        | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag  |
| ---               | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---   |
| C (cysteine)      | +0.249             | 0.0286            | 0.571                | floor |
| S (serine)        | +0.152             | 0.2               | 0.883                |       |
| E (glutamate)     | +0.268             | 0.3429            | 0.883                |       |
| F (phenylalanine) | +0.086             | 0.3429            | 0.883                |       |
| Q (glutamine)     | +0.166             | 0.4857            | 0.883                |       |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (day_5 < day_0)

| amino acid        | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---               | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| H (histidine)     | -0.144             | 0.0571            | 0.571                |      |
| K (lysine)        | -0.192             | 0.2               | 0.883                |      |
| G (glycine)       | -0.193             | 0.3429            | 0.883                |      |
| W (tryptophan)    | -0.252             | 0.4857            | 0.883                |      |
| M (methionine)    | -0.383             | 0.6857            | 0.914                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (day_5 > day_0)

| amino acid        | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---               | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| Q (glutamine)     | +0.482             | 0.1143            | 1.0                  |      |
| S (serine)        | +0.300             | 0.2               | 1.0                  |      |
| E (glutamate)     | +0.212             | 0.3429            | 1.0                  |      |
| N (asparagine)    | +0.040             | 0.3429            | 1.0                  |      |
| C (cysteine)      | +0.131             | 0.4857            | 1.0                  |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (day_5 < day_0)

| amino acid        | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---               | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| P (proline)       | -0.510             | 0.2               | 1.0                  |      |
| W (tryptophan)    | -0.143             | 0.3429            | 1.0                  |      |
| M (methionine)    | -0.170             | 0.4857            | 1.0                  |      |
| A (alanine)       | -0.092             | 0.4857            | 1.0                  |      |
| K (lysine)        | -0.201             | 0.8857            | 1.0                  |      |

### Flag glossary

- `floor` — the row sits at the test's discrete minimum possible raw p-value, given the sample size. For MW two-sided at n=4 vs n=4 this floor is exactly 0.02857. In a 20-AA per-site BH family, one floor hit BH-corrects to `p_adj` = 0.571 — exactly the value observed here at site P, where the floor row (P-C, raw p = 0.0286) is paired with one near-floor row (P-H, raw p = 0.0571) at the same BH wall.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 60
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.5714 (P-site C floor + P-site H near-floor)
- `min raw-p`: 0.02857 (= MW exact floor); 1 row at the floor (P-C +0.249)
- `p_floor`: 0.02857 (n=4 vs n=4 two-sided exact)
- Per-site BH families:
  - A site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.800
  - P site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.571
  - E site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 1.000

## Biological interpretation

**The file shows no statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 across all 60 AA-level day_5-vs-day_0 comparisons, and it is the flattest of the three pairwise contrasts in the `between_timepoint_wilcoxon` family (concrete).** Min raw p across the file is exactly 0.02857 (the n=4 vs n=4 MW exact floor) and the 20-AA per-site BH family puts the wall for a single floor hit at `p_adj` = 0.571. Only site P clears this wall (with one floor row + one near-floor row tied at 0.571); sites A and E sit at min `p_adj` = 0.800 and 1.000 respectively. Site E is *completely* flat — every cell at `p_adj` = 1.0 — which is the strongest "structurally invisible site" signal anywhere in the family. The no-signal result is structural; this contrast carries less signal than its sister contrasts even when re-projected against the same floor.

**P-site Cysteine is the only feature with a floor-level signal in this file (exploratory).** P-C carries +0.249 day_5-enriched at raw p = 0.0286 (the MW exact floor) and BH-corrects to `p_adj` = 0.571 in the 20-AA P-site family, joined by P-H at the same BH wall on the depleted side (-0.144, near-floor). The Headline records the same feature P-C as also flagged in the d10_vs_d0_aa sister, so the natural follow-up question is whether the cysteine signal at P traces a coherent d0->d5->d10 trajectory across the three pairwise contrasts in this family or whether the floor coincidences are structural. The codon-resolution sister at d5_vs_d0 (`between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`) is the cleanest cross-check at this contrast — does it localize the P-C signal to TGC or TGT? And the within-condition Fisher at d5_vs_d0 (`timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`) arbitrates the condition-pooled-confound: if BWM and control individually show coherent P-C direction at d0->d5, the pattern is biological rather than a pooling artefact.

**A-site shows the largest single-cell magnitudes in the file (A-W -0.671, A-N +0.618), but no floor rows (exploratory).** Both are tied at `p_adj` = 0.800 in a 20-AA family — well above the single-floor BH wall of 0.571 — so this is a directional pattern, not a hit. W is a rare amino acid (~0.5% frequency, per the `weighted_log2_enrichment-absent` caveat) so its large |log2_FC| is partly that caveat biting; N is moderate-frequency, so A-N +0.618 is a more stable magnitude. The codon-resolution sister at d5_vs_d0 (`between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`) has not yet localized either to specific synonyms; that is one natural follow-up. The within-condition Fisher sister at the same contrast (`timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`) is the other — a more-powered design that can resolve whether the A-N enrichment and A-W depletion are coherent within each condition or reflect between-condition divergence that the MW pooling here masks.

**E-site is completely flat — the only site in this file to sit at the BH ceiling of `p_adj` = 1.0 (concrete).** Every cell at site E has `p_adj` = 1.0. Even the largest |effect| cells here (E-Q +0.482, E-P -0.510) sit at raw p >= 0.114 — the next discrete step beyond the MW floor for n=4 vs n=4. The Headline records that every site here has weaker signal than in the d10_vs_d5 or d10_vs_d0 file, so combined with the BH ceiling at 1.0 here, E at this contrast is the most-silent (site, contrast) cell in the family. The codon-resolution sister at the same contrast (`between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`) is the natural follow-up — does codon resolution expose any E-site hits that aa-aggregation hides at d5-vs-d0?

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claims to quote: this file shows no significant differences at FDR<0.05 for day_5-vs-day_0 at AA resolution with conditions pooled; the d5-vs-d0 contrast is the flattest contrast in the family per the Headline; site E is completely flat here (every cell at `p_adj` = 1.0). Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) the P-C floor here (+0.249, raw p = 0.0286) paired with the headline's note that the same feature is flagged at d10_vs_d0_aa — does the cysteine signal at P trace a coherent d0->d5->d10 trajectory, or are the floor coincidences structural? The codon-resolution sister (`between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`) and the within-condition Fisher sister (`timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`) are the cleanest cross-checks. (2) A-N (+0.618) and A-W (-0.671) at moderate vs rare frequency — do they anchor coherently within each condition under the more-powered Fisher design at the same contrast? The MW no-signal result here is power-blocked by the n=4 vs n=4 floor, not a biological negative. Noise to ignore: every E-site cell (`p_adj` = 1.0 uniformly) and the large |effect| W-site cells (rare-aa magnitude inflation per the `weighted_log2_enrichment-absent` caveat).

## Methods

MW rank-sum two-sided on per-replicate frequencies, n=4 day_5 (BWM_day5_rep2/3 + control_day5_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_0, BH-FDR per site (each site = 20-AA family). Test answers "do day_5 and day_0 reps differ in per-rep AA frequency at this site?" with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **mw-floor-blocking.** Raw-p is clamped at 0.0286 (n=4 vs n=4 floor); per-site BH wall for one floor hit on a 20-AA family is 0.571. "0/60 at FDR<0.05" here is the design's structural ceiling, not biological evidence of d5 ~= d0.
- **condition-pooled-confound.** Each timepoint's n=4 is 2 BWM + 2 control reps. The P-C floor and the A-N/A-W large effects cannot be attributed to BWM or control here — use `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa` to disentangle.
- **n=4-low-power.** Low power; a no-signal result is weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline.** Headline above explicitly flags 0.0286 as the achievable raw-p minimum.
- **weighted_log2_enrichment-absent.** `log2_FC` of medians only; rare AAs (W ~0.5%, C ~1.5%) get the same column real estate as common AAs (K, E ~10%). A-W (-0.671) and A-Y (-0.412) sit in rare-aa territory — magnitudes inflated by frequency-not-count weighting.
- **small-bh-family-discreteness.** Observed min `p_adj` = 0.571 is exactly the BH wall for one floor hit in a 20-test family. The site-P value (0.571) reflects the floor + near-floor pair landing at the BH discrete-stepping minimum; it is not a sign of underlying mid-strength signal.

### Considered and ruled out

- **asymptotic-with-ties.** Concern that scipy's automatic exact/asymptotic branch choice could distort raw p at low-count cells. Empirically ruled out for this file: of the 60 (site, amino-acid) tests, 0 have rank ties in the pooled 8-element sample, and scipy's `method='auto'` selected the exact branch for all 60 (the pipeline p reconciles to the recomputed exact p within 8e-17). Forcing `method='asymptotic'` for all 60 shifts raw p-values by at most 0.0305 (median 0.0106), flips 0 raw-p<0.05 decisions, and leaves 0 hits at FDR<0.05 in either branch (asymptotic per-site min p_adj A/P/E = 0.776 / 0.606 / 1.000 vs the as-shipped exact 0.800 / 0.571 / 1.000). The branch choice does not change any conclusion in this report.

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site day_5-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (N, D, I, M, V, E, Q, T, F, R, K, S, G, P, L, C, H, A, Y, W). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-0.7 to +0.65. Largest enrichment at N (~+0.62); largest depletion at W (~-0.67, rare-aa territory). No floor rows visible — site A min p_adj = 0.800.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d5_vs_d0/individual/site_A_aa_Day5_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![P-site day_5-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (E, C, Q, S, F, Y, N, V, I, D, A, L, P, R, T, H, K, G, W, M). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-0.4 to +0.27. Largest enrichments at E (~+0.27) and C (~+0.25, floor row); largest depletion at M (~-0.38). Floor visible at C on the enriched side and near-floor at H mid-pack on the depleted side.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d5_vs_d0/individual/site_P_aa_Day5_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![E-site day_5-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (Q, S, E, C, Y, T, G, N, I, H, L, V, D, R, F, A, W, M, K, P). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-0.51 to +0.48. Largest enrichment at Q (~+0.48); largest depletion at P (~-0.51). Site is completely flat at the BH-correction level — every cell at p_adj = 1.0 despite plot-visible |log2_FC| values up to 0.51. Highest min p_adj of any site in any file of the family.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d5_vs_d0/individual/site_E_aa_Day5_vs_Day0_barplot.png)
